questions.question: What is the correct order of the basic container lifecycle?
What signal does 'docker stop' send first to a container?
How long does 'docker stop' wait before sending SIGKILL by default?
What does 'docker kill' do to a container?
options: Sends SIGTERM and waits for a graceful shutdown
Removes the container and its image
Force stops the container immediately with SIGKILL
Pauses all processes in the container
questions.answer: Force stops the container immediately with SIGKILL

questions.question: What command is used to start a previously stopped container?
What status does a container show in 'docker ps' when it has been paused?
Which command resumes a paused container?
What happens to a container's uptime when you run 'docker restart'?
options: The uptime continues from where it was
The uptime resets because the container is fully stopped and restarted
The uptime is not tracked after a restart
questions.answer: The uptime resets because the container is fully stopped and restarted

questions.question: Which command shows only stopped containers?
What does 'docker logs my-app' display?
options: The Dockerfile used to build the image
All output from the container since it started
Only error messages from the container
questions.answer: All output from the container since it started

questions.question: Which flag shows only the last 5 lines of container logs?
What does the '-f' flag do when used with 'docker logs'?
options: Follows the log output in real time (live stream)
Finds a specific string in the logs
questions.answer: Follows the log output in real time (live stream)

questions.question: Which flag shows logs from the last 1 minute?
What does 'docker exec' do?
options: Executes a Dockerfile to build an image
Runs a command inside a running container
Starts a new container from an image
questions.answer: Runs a command inside a running container

questions.question: What flags are needed to open an interactive shell inside a running container?
What happens to a running container when you exit an 'exec' session?
Which command copies a file from a container to the host?
What happens to changes made inside a container if the container is removed and recreated?
options: The changes are saved to the image
The changes are lost because they are ephemeral
The changes are backed up to the host
questions.answer: The changes are lost because they are ephemeral

questions.question: What does 'docker container prune' do?
What does 'docker system df' show?
options: The Dockerfile for the current directory
Disk usage by images, containers, and volumes
A list of all Docker commands
questions.answer: Disk usage by images, containers, and volumes

questions.question: What does 'docker system prune' do?
options: Removes all unused containers, networks, and dangling images
Removes all Docker data including running containers
questions.answer: Removes all unused containers, networks, and dangling images

questions.question: Can you stop multiple containers in a single command?
options: No, you must stop them one at a time
Yes, by listing their names: docker stop app1 app2 app3
Yes, but only with docker kill
Yes, but only with docker container prune
questions.answer: Yes, by listing their names: docker stop app1 app2 app3

questions.question: Which command can you use to remove a stopped container?
What does the '-d' flag do in 'docker run -d --name my-app nginx'?
options: Deletes the container after it stops
Runs the container in detached mode (background)
questions.answer: Runs the container in detached mode (background)

questions.question: Which command copies a file from the host to a running container?
